notes: Third production Level 2 run, Candidate B from the cal.com triage candidate analysis. First Cursor agent population (100% Cursor), small TypeScript monorepo (apps/* + packages/* layout), 40 PRs. Completes the cross-agent triangle: cal.com (Devin/monorepo), remix-forms (Codex/library), this (Cursor/monorepo). Skip rate: 0% — best of any scan so far. cal.com 81% / remix-forms 21% / this 0% confirms parent SHA preservation is agent-specific (Devin worst, Codex middle, Cursor best). Finding rate 72.5% (29/40 PRs) — highest of any scan, exceeding cal.com's 64.3%. 23 F9 findings split: 10 'search string not found' (X-90 fabrication territory), 10 'file not found' (suspect SI-003 multi-commit FP class — confirms SI-003 fires on Cursor too, not just Devin, supporting the multi-commit-pattern hypothesis over agent-specific hypothesis), 3 'ambiguous match' (X-91 territory). 5 serialization findings: 4 on apps/internal-cli/package.json with 'JSON Parse error: Unexpected identifier lockfileVersion' (lockfileVersion is the top-level field of pnpm-lock/yarn.lock/package-lock files — the scanner reconstructed an edit to a file path of 'package.json' but the patch content is actually lockfile content; second occurrence of the Candidate 4 serialization-misfire class from cal.com triage, now at ≥3 total occurrences and promotable to a SCANNER-INCIDENT after triage). 1 serialization on PHASE_1_COMPLETION_REPORT.md (third pattern variation: serialization predicate firing on .md file, suggests auto-predicate generator or diff parser bug). 1 access finding on ALONGSIDE_FLAG_FEATURE.md (path traversal in a Markdown file — same gates-on-wrong-file-type theme). All findings unknown or low confidence; zero high-confidence findings on this scan. Per-PR JSONL stays Lenovo-local per data-separate-knowledge-unified policy. Triage pending operator review.
